componentDescription: Avatar displays a visual representation of a user or entity — either an image, initials derived from a name, or a fallback icon. Use Avatar wherever you need to visually identify a person: comments, user lists, assignee pickers, profile headers, and notification items.
features: Three content modes: image URL, initials (auto-derived from name), or icon fallback
Multiple size variants for different UI contexts
Circular shape by default with optional square variant
Color-coded initials backgrounds when no image is provided
Status indicator dot overlay (online, offline, busy)

usageExamples.title: User avatar with image
usageExamples.description: Avatar showing a user profile photo.

usageExamples.title: Initials avatar when no image is available
usageExamples.description: Avatar that shows initials when no image src is provided.

usageExamples.title: Avatar in a comment thread
usageExamples.description: Small avatar used inline in a comment list.

{comment.authorName}

{comment.text}

src.llmContext: URL of the user's profile image. When provided, the image is displayed. If the image fails to load, falls back to initials (if name is provided) or a default icon.

name.llmContext: The user's full name. Used to auto-generate initials when no image is available (e.g., 'Sarah Johnson' → 'SJ'). Also used as the alt text for the image for accessibility.
name.propDescription: User name for initials fallback and accessibility

size.llmContext: Use 'xs' or 'sm' in dense lists, table cells, and inline mentions. Use 'md' for comment threads, cards, and list items. Use 'lg' or 'xl' for profile headers and prominent user displays.
